subject: Chemistry
q: Diamond has a three dimensional structure of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> atoms formed by covalent bonds. The structure of diamond has face centred cubic lattice where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of the tetrahedral voids are also occupied by carbon atoms. The number of carbon atoms present per unit cell of diamond is ________.
question: Diamond has a three dimensional structure of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> atoms formed by covalent bonds. The structure of diamond has face centred cubic lattice where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of the tetrahedral voids are also occupied by carbon atoms. The number of carbon atoms present per unit cell of diamond is ________.
solution:
Carbon atoms occupy FCC lattice points as well as half of the tetrahedral voids

therefore number of carbon atoms atoms per unit cell =8

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following exists as covalent crystals in the solid state ?
question: Which of the following exists as covalent crystals in the solid state ?
solution:
Silicon is covalent or network solid.

Each Si atom is in sp3 hybridisation.

Sulphur (S8)  –  molecular solid.

Phosphorus (P4)  – molecular solid.

Iodine (I2)   –  molecular solid.

subject: Chemistry
q: Na and Mg crystallize in BCC and FCC type crystals respectively, then the number of atoms of Na and Mg present in the unit cell of their respective crystal is
question: Na and Mg crystallize in BCC and FCC type crystals respectively, then the number of atoms of Na and Mg present in the unit cell of their respective crystal is
solution:
BCC - points are at corners and one in the centre of the unit cell
Number of atoms per unit cell $=8 \times \frac{1}{8}+1=2$
FCC - points are at the corners and also centre of the six faces of each cell
Number of atoms per unit cell $=8 \times \frac{1}{8}+6 \times \frac{1}{2}=4$

subject: Chemistry
q: Atoms of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> form <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>hcp</mi></math> lattice and those of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> occupy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math> of its tetrahedral voids. The percentage of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> in the lattice is (Nearest integer)
question: Atoms of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> form <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>hcp</mi></math> lattice and those of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> occupy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math> of its tetrahedral voids. The percentage of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> in the lattice is (Nearest integer)
solution:
Number of effective atoms in HCP lattice is =6

Number of atoms of X=6

Number of T.V. in h.c.p. =12 2×Number of atoms

Number of Y atoms =23×12=8 atoms.

% of X=614×100=42.86%

Round off to the nearest integer = 43

subject: Chemistry
q:
A metal M forms hexagonal close-packed structure. The total number of voids in 0.02 mol of it is _____ ×1021 (Nearest integer)

(Given NA=6.02×1023)
question:
A metal M forms hexagonal close-packed structure. The total number of voids in 0.02 mol of it is _____ ×1021 (Nearest integer)

(Given NA=6.02×1023)
solution:
From the diagram of voids in HCP:

There are 6 octahedral voids and 12 tetrahedral voids in a HCP unit cell.
One unit cell of HCP contains =18 voids

Thus, No. of voids in 0.02 mol of hcp

=186×6.02×1023×0.02

≈3.6×1022

≈36×1021

subject: Chemistry
q: How many unit cells are present in a cubeshaped ideal crystal of $\mathrm{NaCl}$ of mass $1.00 \mathrm{~g}$ ? [Atomic masses: $\mathrm{Na}=23, \mathrm{Cl}=35.5$ ]
question: How many unit cells are present in a cubeshaped ideal crystal of $\mathrm{NaCl}$ of mass $1.00 \mathrm{~g}$ ? [Atomic masses: $\mathrm{Na}=23, \mathrm{Cl}=35.5$ ]
solution:
Number of formulas in cube shaped crystals $=\frac{1.0}{58.5} \times 6.02 \times 10^{23}$ since in $\mathrm{NaCl}$ type of structure 4 formula units form a cell
$\therefore$ unit cells $=\frac{1.0 \times 6.02 \times 10^{23}}{58.5 \times 4}=2.57 \times 10^{21}$ unit cells.

subject: Chemistry
q: Consider the bcc unit cells of the solids 1 and 2 with the position of atoms as shown below. The radius of atom B  is twice that of atom A. The unit cell edge length is 50 % more in solid 2 than in 1 . What is the approximate packing efficiency in solid 2 ?
question: Consider the bcc unit cells of the solids 1 and 2 with the position of atoms as shown below. The radius of atom B  is twice that of atom A. The unit cell edge length is 50 % more in solid 2 than in 1 . What is the approximate packing efficiency in solid 2 ?
solution:
If radius of A is r radius of B will be 2r . Atoms in the body diagonal touch.

Therefore:
r+2r+2r+r=3 a

6 r=3 a or a=6r3

Packing Efficiency
=1×43πr3+1×43π(2r)3a3=43πr3×9(6r/3)3
=4×9×π×r3×333×216×r3=90.5 %

subject: Chemistry
q:
The radius of the largest sphere which fits properly at the centre of the edge of a body centred cubic unit cell is :

(Edge length is represented by 'a')
question:
The radius of the largest sphere which fits properly at the centre of the edge of a body centred cubic unit cell is :

(Edge length is represented by 'a')
solution:
For bcc

$$

\Rightarrow \mathrm{R}=\frac{\sqrt{3} \mathrm{a}}{4}

$$

$$

\therefore \text { Empty space at edge }=a-2 R=a-\frac{\sqrt{3} a}{2}

$$

$=$ diameter of sphere.

$$

\therefore \quad \mathrm{r}_{\text {sphere }}=\frac{a-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \mathrm{a}}{2}=\left(\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4}\right) \mathrm{a}=0.067 \mathrm{a}

$$

subject: Chemistry
q: In a face centered cubic lattice atoms A are at the corner points and atoms B at the face centered points. If atom B is missing from one of the face centered points, the formula of the ionic compound is :
question: In a face centered cubic lattice atoms A are at the corner points and atoms B at the face centered points. If atom B is missing from one of the face centered points, the formula of the ionic compound is :
solution:
A atoms at corners  = 8 × 1 8 =  A (one atom)

B atom is missing at One face centre then will present at 5 faces

At face centre atom contribute 1/2 part

Number of B atom at Face centre = 5× 1 2 (B atom)

So formula of compound = A2B5

subject: Chemistry
q: The total number of octahedral void (s) per atom present in a cubic close packed structure is :
question: The total number of octahedral void (s) per atom present in a cubic close packed structure is :
solution:
In CCP there are four atoms per unit cell octahedral voids are

12Edge centres14+1Body centre=4 O.V. [Contribution at edge centre is14 ]

Four octahedral voids are present in a unit cell i.e. four atoms.

Therefore, per atom one octahedral void will be present.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Iron oxide FeO, crystallises in a cubic lattice with a unit cell edge length of 5.0Å. If density of the FeO in the crystal is 4.0 g cm-3, then the number of FeO units present per unit cell is _____ (Nearest integer)

Given : Molar mass of Fe and O is 56 and 16 g mol-1 respectively.
NA=6.0×1023 mol-1
question:
Iron oxide FeO, crystallises in a cubic lattice with a unit cell edge length of 5.0Å. If density of the FeO in the crystal is 4.0 g cm-3, then the number of FeO units present per unit cell is _____ (Nearest integer)

Given : Molar mass of Fe and O is 56 and 16 g mol-1 respectively.
NA=6.0×1023 mol-1
solution:
The density of the crystal can be calculated as follows,

d=Z×MN0×a3

d=densityZ=effective number of atomsM=Molar massN0=Avogadro's numbera=Edge length

4=Z×726×1023×125×10-24

Z=4.166≃4

q:
At 100oC,  copper Cu has FCC unit cell structure with cell edge length of x Å . What is the approximate density of Cu (in g cm-3 ) at this temperature?

Atomic Mass of Cu=63.55 u
question:
At 100oC,  copper Cu has FCC unit cell structure with cell edge length of x Å . What is the approximate density of Cu (in g cm-3 ) at this temperature?

Atomic Mass of Cu=63.55 u
solution:
Crystal structure is FCC, so number of atoms per unit cell N=4

Edge length =x.Å=x 10-8  cm3

Density ZMNAa3=4×63.56.03×1023x×10-83gm/cm3

=4×63.56.0×x3×10-1=422x3gm/cm3=422x3

subject: Chemistry
q:
Select the correct statements.
(A) Crystalline solids have long range order.
(B) Crystalline solids are isotropic.
(C) Amorphous solid are sometimes called pseudo solids.
(D) Amorphous solids soften over a range of
temperatures.
(E) Amorphous solids have a definite heat of fusion. Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
question:
Select the correct statements.
(A) Crystalline solids have long range order.
(B) Crystalline solids are isotropic.
(C) Amorphous solid are sometimes called pseudo solids.
(D) Amorphous solids soften over a range of
temperatures.
(E) Amorphous solids have a definite heat of fusion. Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
solution:
(A) Crystalline solids have definite arrangement of constituent particles and have long range order.
(C), (D) Different constituent particles of an amorphous solid have different bond strengths and soften over a range of temperatures.
